prose:
Electrostatics — GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied methylamine.xyz (7 atoms); single-point electrostatics evaluation, no geometry relaxation (electrostatics//input-XYZ).
SCF converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy true; no engine warnings.
Dipole moment magnitude = 1.4945174 D (dipole vector [0.34446906, 0.13029246, 1.4484293] D). Partial charges: Mulliken (GFN2-xTB) scheme summing to neutral.
Command run: cli.py electrostatics --method xtb methylamine.xyz --out <live.json> (xtb v. as bundled in chemkit). The engine wrote a live log to electrostatics_<timestamp>.out in the working directory.
Method provenance block:
• Property: Dipole moment (Debye)
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical, no Gaussian basis)
• Geometry: supplied XYZ, single-point (no optimisation)
• Solvent: gas phase (no implicit model)
• Charge/mult: 0/1 (closed-shell singlet)
• Software: xtb via chemkit
• Convergence: SCF converged, integrity trust ✓

No caveats beyond the general accuracy regime of GFN2-xTB (reliable for trends, not chemical-accuracy absolutes).
